---
title: "Section 1 Assignment PDF"
author: "Instructor Michael Downey"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

# Section 1: Import and Communicate Exercise

Enter your information below:

Data 332 Section \# **02**

Student name: **My Ha**

**References**

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

Visual Editing in RStudio – Quarto: <https://quarto.org/docs/visual-editor/>

Datacamp Quarto Cheat Sheet: <https://media.datacamp.com/legacy/image/upload/v1676540721/Marketing/Blog/Quarto_Cheat_Sheet.pdf>

# Set your working Directory and load files

Create a new directory using your computer’s file manager.

In RStudio: From the top Ribbon, pick Session: set working Directory, Open the directory with the files you created above

Use getwd() in the Console to verify your working directory is now set.

Section 1 has its focus in the Import and Communicate Phases of this methodology. Bringing data in from the World and being able to communicate the results of Data Analytics completed within R are critical steps to beginning an effective knowledge in R.\

![Figure 1: Courtesy: R4DS - Hadley et al.](whole-game-image.png)

This image is stored in the Students Location and can be inserted in this docment as above. It is center justified, the size has been changed, and the acknowledgement inserted.

# Packages and Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
```

# Download Data332_Section 1 Assignment.xlsx from the Student Location at this link:

<https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1g60OSGX.JnF3Q97MBD9BT1M4.JmPLfJ6rd>\
Ensuring to copy it to the working directory you have created for this assignment.\
Read the .xlsx and assign it to a variable in the .qmd you create for this assignment as below:

```{r}
# read xlsx and assign to a variable
xlsx_file<-read_xlsx("Data332_Section 1 Assignment.xlsx")
#
# Print the File to confirm
print (read_xlsx("Data332_Section 1 Assignment.xlsx"))
```

```{r}
#View the File
xlsx_file
```

```{r}
# The top row are the column names and the data types for each column
# shown below with "< >" designations


#Check Global environment for the file and verify contents
```

# List the Data Types in this file

```{r}
str(xlsx_file)
```

The file above is readable but here is an easier way to read the data types.

Let's stored the datatypes to document their types.

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(purrr)

Excel_datatypes <- tibble( column = names(xlsx_file),
type = map_chr(xlsx_file,~
paste(class(.x), collapse = ", ")))
print (Excel_datatypes)
```

This format is much easier to understand and will prove as a good reference ass your analysis progresses.

# Download Data332_Section 1 Assignment(CSV).csv from the Student Location at this link:

<https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1g6OOSGX.JnF3Q97MBD9BT1M4.JmPLfJ6rd> Ensuring to copy it to the working directory you have created for this assignment. Read the .CSV and assign it to a variable in the .qmd you create for this assignment as below:

```{r}
csv_file<-read_csv("Data332_Section 1 Assignment(CSV).csv")
```

```{r}
print (csv_file)
```

```{r}
CSV_datatypes <- tibble( column = names (csv_file),
type = map_chr(csv_file, ~
paste(class(.x), collapse = ", ")) )
print (CSV_datatypes)
```

Note: Which Datatype was lost in the CSV file....???

# Moodle Submission

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code.

# Submit for the assignment:

1\) the .qmd file used to create

2\) the render .pdf
